data.title: Celestica tumbles even as Q1 results, guidance top estimates
data.description:
Celestica (CLS) shares tumbled 7% in extended trading on Monday even as the Canadian technology firm reported first-quarter results and guidance that topped estimates.

For the period ending March 31, Celestica said it earned an adjusted $2.16 per share as revenue rose 52.8% year-over-year to come in at $4.05B. Analysts had expected the company to earn an adjusted $2.08 per share on $4.05B.

“Celestica delivered a strong first quarter to begin 2026, achieving revenue of $4.05 billion and adjusted EPS (non-GAAP) of $2.16. Our solid results included adjusted operating margin of 8.0%, a new milestone for the company,” said Rob Mionis, President and CEO, in a statement. “We continue to see accelerating growth from our CCS customer base, alongside increasing profitability in both our CCS and ATS segments. Driven by this momentum, we are raising our 2026 annual outlook to $19.0 billion in revenue and $10.15 in adjusted EPS (non-GAAP). Our outlook for 2027 also continues to strengthen from just 90 days ago, supported by new program wins as well as improved forecast visibility with our customers.”

Looking to the second-quarter, Celestica expects to earn between $2.14 and $2.34 per share on an adjusted basis, above the $2.13 per share estimate. Sales are forecast to be between $4.15B and $4.45B, with the midpoint above the $4.18B estimate.

The company will host a conference call at 8 a.m. on Tuesday to discuss the results.

data.title: Morocco Strategic Minerals upsizes private placement to $7.5M
data.description:
• Morocco Strategic Minerals (MCC:CA) increased its non-brokered private placement to $7.5M from $5M.
  • The offering includes up to 50M units priced at $0.15/unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and half a warrant.
  • Each full warrant allows purchase of one share at $0.25 within 24 months.

data.title: Nucor Non-GAAP EPS of $0.77, revenue of $9.5B beats by $640M
data.description:
• Nucor press release (NUE): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.77.
  • Revenue of $9.5B (+21.3% Y/Y) beats by $640M.
  • Consolidated net earnings attributable to Nucor stockholders of $743 million, or $3.23 per diluted share, for the first quarter of 2026.

  • On February 20, 2026, Nucor's Board of Directors declared a cash dividend of $0.56 per share. This cash dividend is payable on May 11, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 31, 2026 and is Nucor's 212th consecutive quarterly cash dividend.

data.title: Gulf Resources receives Nasdaq notice over delayed annual filing
data.description:
• Gulf Resources (GURE) on Monday said it has received a delinquency notice from Nasdaq after failing to timely file its annual report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • The company said the notice does not immediately affect its Nasdaq listing.
  • Gulf Resources said it has 60 days to submit a plan to regain compliance and could be granted up to 180 days, until October 12, 2026, to file the report if its plan is accepted. If not, it may appeal the decision.
  • The company said it is working to complete the filing.
  • GURE +0.24% after hours to $3.5386.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stocks to watch on Monday after hours: CDNS, BBBY, AMKR
data.description:
Wall Street ended mixed on Monday as investors braced for a pivotal week—eyeing Big Tech earnings, the upcoming Federal Reserve decision, and ongoing developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended +0.1%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed +0.2%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished -0.1%.

Now, here are the 3 stocks to watch on Monday after hours:

  • Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) shares rose 2.8% in late trading on Monday after the electronic design automation firm posted first-quarter results that were above Wall Street's forecast.
  • Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) recorded its first quarter of significant revenue growth in almost five years, with an increase of 9.4% year after year after adjusting for the retailer's exit from Canada. During the quarter, the company saw better engagement, stronger conversion, and improvements in average order value. Shares jumped 20% in extended trading.
  • Amkor Technology (AMKR) shares declined 1.5% even after the semiconductor packaging and test services company reported earnings per share of $0.33 versus the consensus estimate of $0.24.

data.title: FB Financial authorizes $175M share repurchase program
data.description:
• FB Financial (FBK) on Monday said its board of directors has approved a new authorization to repurchase up to $175 million of its common stock.
  • The program will remain in effect until June 30, 2027, replacing a prior authorization set to expire in January 2027.
  • The company said repurchases may be made through open market purchases or private transactions, depending on market conditions and other factors including regulatory requirements and capital needs.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: agilon health appoints new CEO
data.description:
• agilon health (AGL) named Tim O’Rourke as chief executive officer and board member, effective May 7.
  • O’Rourke will succeed Ronald A. Williams, who will remain chairman of the board.
